rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIKE ANGEL LEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver With Pos
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Now before the Court is Petitioner and Administrator Evelia Rojas Leon's Motion for Appointment of Receiver. Petitioner's unopposed request for judicial notice is GRANTED. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Decedent Mike Angel Leon died May 17, 2019. Petitioner filed this motion in November 2021. On November 16, 2021, the Court issued Temporary Restraining Order, retraining Respondent or any agent thereof from interfering with Petitioner's access as Administrator of the estate to the business records of Eagle Bar, LLC. The Opposition to this Motion was filed on March 3, 2022. On March 4, 2022, Order Granting Issuance of Preliminary Injunction was entered. It, in part, set forth a schedule and parameters for Petitioner to examine the files and records of San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC, as the personal representative of the deceased co-owner, Mike Angel Leon. After Respondent's counsel objected to Petitioner's counsel's request to continue the August 10, 20222 hearing on this Motion, Petitioner filed a Reply filed August 4, 2022. Decedent, Mike Angel Leon, and Jesus Alejandro Montiel Urbana were co-venturers in a limited liability corporation organized on August 15, 2011 as Double Rainbow, LLC, and amended on January 9, 2013 to San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC (entity number 201122810033). In 2018, Decedent filed a civil action to partition the LLC, and Montiel filed a cross-complaint to enforce the LLC's Operating Agreement's buy-out provision. On May 17, 2019, Mike Leon died. On September 18, 2019, Montiel unilaterally filed a Certificate of Cancelation to dissolve San Francisco Eagle Bar LLC (entity number 201122810033). On January 27, 2021, Montiel registered San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC (entity number 202103210383), listing himself as sole owner. Petitioner was appointed Administrator of Decedent's estate and Letters issued on August 18, 2020. It undisputed that Montiel has continued to operate the underlying business, The Eagle Bar. Petitioner alleges that Montiel has wrongly refused her access to the business records and is endangering the estate's property interest via mismanagement of the business and self-dealing, including engaging in litigation ultimately for his individual benefit, all of which necessitates a receiver to be appointed immediately. In the Reply filed August 4, 2022, Petitioner further updates the court with information discovered since the original motion papers were filed in November 2021, including: Respondent filed or caused to be filed Federal and California tax returns for the years 2019 and 2020 listing Decedent as a 50% owner and general partner of the business, after Decedent's death, and without notice to the estate or any personal representative of the estate; Respondent reported on W-2 forms that it paid Decedent in 2019 and 2020, without notice to the estate or any personal representative of the estate; Respondent or counsel for Respondent has withheld or delayed production of documents encompassed by the November 16, 2021 Temporary Restraining Order and the March 4, 2022 Order Granting Issuance of Preliminary Injunctions. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIKE ANGEL LEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel San Francisco Eagle Bar, Llc To Produce, Without Objection, U.S. Bank Statements Requested In Subpoena (Duces Tecum) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Thomas Smurro
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2 (US Bank)] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Now before the Court is Petitioner and Administrator Evelia Rojas Leon's Motion to Compel San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC to Produce, Without Objection, US Bank Statements Requested in Subpoena (Duces Tecum), and Request for Monetary Sanctions Against Thomas Smurro. Petitioner brings this motions under Code of Civil Procedure sections 1987.1(a), 1987.2, 2025.480; California Rules of Court, rule 3.1348(a); Corporations Code sections 17704.10, 17705.02, and 17705.04, 17702.13(d), 17706.02. Decedent Mike Angel Leon died May 17, 2019. Petitioner was appointed Administrator of the Estate of Mike Angel Leon by order of This Court on July 30, 2020. Letters were issued on August 18, 2020. On September 1, 2021, Petitioner served a Subpoena Duces Tecum on the Custodian of Records for US Bank, for the production of all bank statements held in the LLC's name from January 2, 2018 through present day, including for account ending in xx0237. (Kelly Decl. at par. 6, Ex. C). Respondent sent a letter to the subpoenaed party, objecting to the subpoena and directing the subpoenaed party to not produce any records absent a court order, and stating several conditions for production. (Kelly Decl. at par. 8, Ex. E; Smurro Decl at par. 5.) Petitioner filed this motion in November 2021. On November 16, 2021, the Court issued Temporary Restraining Order, retraining Respondent or any agent thereof from interfering with Petitioner's access as Administrator of the estate to the business records of Eagle Bar, LLC. The Opposition to this Motion was filed on March 3, 2022. On March 4, 2022, Order Granting Issuance of Preliminary Injunction was entered. It, in part, set forth a schedule and parameters for Petitioner to examine the files and records of San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC, as the personal representative of the deceased co-owner, Mike Angel Leon. After Respondent's counsel objected to Petitioner's counsel's request to continue the August 10, 2022 hearing on this Motion, Petitioner filed a Reply filed August 4, 2022. The Opposition to this Motion states Petitioner received the subpoenaed documents prior to filing this motion. Smurro's declaration in support dated February 25, 2022 states at paragraph 9: "U.S. Bank promptly sent me a letter confirming that attorney Kelly received the records on September 21, 2021, with a copy of the shipping label and a confirmation of receipt. A true copy of that letter, dated December 13, 2021, is attached to this declaration as Exhibit 8 and incorporated by this reference." Exhibit 8 is a letter from US Bank to Smurro, listing eight credit cards accounts and two deposit accounts; the account numbers are entirely redacted in the copy before the court. According to the Reply filed August 4, 2022, "On September 21, 2021, US Bank caused various Credit Card Statements and two deposit account statements to be delivered to The Kelly Law Firm; however, the sole US Bank Account Statements propounded in the US Bank Subpoena was for US Bank Statement ending in xx0237. The requested LLC account was not produced in US Bank's submission, nor has the LLC produced the same. At the time this Reply is filed, the sole US Bank Account Statements requested by Petitioner (account xx0237) have not been produced by either US Bank or LLC." (Reply at 2:6-10.) [Part 1 of 2 (US Bank)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIKE ANGEL LEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel San Francisco Eagle Bar, Llc To Produce, Without Objection, Wells Fargo Bank Statements Requested In Subpoena (Duces Tecum) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Thomas Smurro
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2 (Wells Fargo)] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Now before the Court is Petitioner and Administrator Evelia Rojas Leon's Motion to Compel San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC to Produce, Without Objection, Wells Fargo Bank Statements Requested in Subpoena (Duces Tecum), and Request for Monetary Sanctions Against Thomas Smurro. Petitioner brings this motions under Code of Civil Procedure sections1987.1(a), 1987.2, 2025.480; California Rules of Court, rule 3.1348(a); Corporations Code sections 17704.10, 17705.02, and 17705.04, 17702.13(d), 17706.02. Decedent Mike Angel Leon died May 17, 2019. Petitioner was appointed Administrator of the Estate of Mike Angel Leon by order of This Court on July 30, 2020. Letters were issued on August 18, 2020. On September 1, 2021, Petitioner served a Subpoena Duces Tecum on the Custodian of Records for Wells Fargo, for the production of all bank statements held in the LLC's name from January 2, 2018 through present day, including for accounts ending in xx7722, xx6880, xx8127, and xx6816. (Kelly Decl. at par. 6, Ex. C). Respondent sent a letter to the subpoenaed party, objecting to the subpoena and directing the subpoenaed party to not produce any records absent a court order, and stating several conditions for production. (Kelly Decl. at par. 8, Ex. E; Smurro Decl at par. 5.) Petitioner filed this motion in November 2021. On November 16, 2021, the Court issued Temporary Restraining Order, retraining Respondent or any agent thereof from interfering with Petitioner's access as Administrator of the estate to the business records of Eagle Bar, LLC. The Opposition to this Motion was filed on March 3, 2022. On March 4, 2022, Order Granting Issuance of Preliminary Injunction was entered. It, in part, set forth a schedule and parameters for Petitioner to examine the files and records of San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC, as the personal representative of the deceased co-owner, Mike Angel Leon. After Respondent's counsel objected to Petitioner's counsel's request to continue the August 10, 20222 hearing on this Motion, Petitioner filed a Reply filed August 4, 2022. The Opposition to this Motion states "The Estate received the subpoenaed records from Wells Fargo Bank on December 2, 2021." (Opp. at 3:16-17.) Smurro's declaration in support dated February 25, 2022 states at paragraph 12: "Wells Fargo Bank has been nowhere as helpful. They told me on the telephone they had sent a copy of the subpoenaed records to attorney Kelly on December 2, 2021. I asked for a written confirmation of that fact from Wells Fargo Bank but, unlike with U.S. Bank, which had promptly complied, I still do not have any written confirmation of the date the records were produced, although I called as far up the telephone chain as I could and then went to my bank branch and asked for their help." Smurro states at paragraph 13 of this declaration, "Attorney Kelly will be able to confirm when she received the records from both banks." According to the Reply filed August 4, 2022, "to date, neither Wells Fargo, nor Objector, has produced Wells Fargo Statements ending in xx6880 or xx6816." (Reply at 2:18-19.) [Part 1 of 2 (Wells Fargo)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIKE ANGEL LEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver With Pos (Added For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] Petitioner has shown by a preponderance of the evidence that Decedent had a joint interest in the San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC, and that the estate's interest in that property is in danger of loss or material injury. Although Respondent disputes the amount of interest the estate holds in the LLC, its arguments acknowledge the estate has some interest (See, e.g., Opp. at 3:11-15, and Montiel Decl. at par. 6, re buy-out attempts; Opp. at 4:1-5:23, re liability owed to LLC, and civil actions for dissolution and to enforce Buyout Agreement.) Petition has shown by a preponderance of the evidence that Decedent had a joint interest in the LLC (see, Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. D, August 17, 2021 Breach of Contract, Specific Performance and Quiet Title action in Case No. CGC-21-593995. Decl. of Kelly ISO Reply, Ex. A, B, C, D, E). As of this motion being filed in November 2021, and then updated in the August 4, 2022 Reply, the state of the original LLC's assets is unknown to the Administrator but appear to be solely under Montiel's possession and control. The record before the Court shows by a preponderance of the evidence that the estate's interest is in danger of loss or material injury, either because Montiel has refused to acknowledge the estate's interest in the property and is operating the business as if he is the sole owner; or because Montiel unilaterally dissolved the original LLC after Decedent's death and before a personal representative of the estate had been appointed and then registered a new LLC with the same name, listing himself as the sole owner and retaining possession and control of the original LLC's assets; or because Montiel has filed Federal and California tax forms, after Decedent's death, as if Decedent were both still alive and a 50% owner and general partner of the original LLC. Given the preponderance of the evidence presented in the papers, and the undisputed facts - including Respondent's continued delay in producing discovery and other records the estate is entitled to - the court finds a less severe alternative to receivership would not adequately address and protect the rights of both parties. Petitioner's Motion for Appointment of Receiver is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 564(b)(1). The receiver has, under the control of the Court, the powers enumerated in Code of Civil Procedure section 568. Insofar as the motion requests relief other than the appointment of a receiver, those requests are DENIED without prejudice. Respondent's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIKE ANGEL LEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion To Compel San Francisco Eagle Bar, Llc To Produce, Without Objection, Wells Fargo Bank Statements Requested In Subpoena (Duces Tecum) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Thomas Smurro (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2 (Wells Fargo)] Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1987.1(a), "If a subpoena requires [] the production of books, documents, electronically stored information, or other things before a court, or at the trial of an issue therein, or at the taking of a deposition, the court, upon motion reasonably made by any person described in subdivision (b), [] may make an order quashing the subpoena entirely, modifying it, or directing compliance with it upon those terms or conditions as the court shall declare, including protective orders." Petitioner's Motion to Compel San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC to Produce, Without Objection, Wells Fargo Bank Statements Requested in Subpoena (Duces Tecum) is GRANTED. Wells Fargo Bank is directed to comply with the Subpoena Duces Tecum Petitioner served on the Custodian of Records for Wells Fargo on September 1, 2021, for the production of all bank statements held in the name of Eagle Bar, LLC, for accounts ending in xx7722, xx6880, xx8127, and xx6816, from January 2, 2018 through present day, within 15 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Respondent's request in the Opposition to Motion for sanctions to be awarded against Petitioner and Petitioner's counsel is DENIED. Petitioner's Request for Monetary Sanctions Against Thomas Smurro is GRANTED, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1987.2 and California Rules of Court, rule 3.1348(a). The record does not demonstrate that Smurro acted with substantial justification in opposing this motion or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. This motion to compel production of discoverable records was necessitated by Smurro's, as Respondent's counsel, unreasonable interference with the Administrator's efforts to obtain the LLC's financial information directly from US Bank as an asset of the estate and as part of the personal representative's duty to inventory and account for estate assets. The Administrator's subpoena to US Bank was itself necessitated by Respondent's unreasonable refusal, through Smurro as counsel for Respondent, to produce LLC business and financial records to the personal representative of the deceased co-owner. Sanctions on this Motion to Compel San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC to Produce, Without Objection, US Bank Statements Requested in Subpoena (Duces Tecum) are set at $2,000. Smurro is to pay Petitioner $2,000 within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2 (Wells Fargo)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIKE ANGEL LEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver With Pos
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] The current preliminary injunction restrains and enjoins Montiel and any agent of the LLC from: "Moving, transferring, relocating, deleting, or destroying any business records of San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC, including, but not limited to: financial statements, tax returns, lists of assets, forecasts and projections, business plans and organization documents, photos of the facility and equipment, appraisals, leases, transactions documents, files, books, emails, receipts, payroll information, profit and loss statements, valuations, account statements, etc." It does not order an accounting of the business or restrain Montiel from operating the business or using business assets. The motion argues that this lack of oversight is endangering the estate's interest in the business, and that Montiel may be self-dealing or otherwise mismanaging the business. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 564(b) "A receiver may be appointed by the court in which an action or proceeding is pending, or by a judge of that court, in the following cases[.]" What follows are the exclusive circumstances under which the court may appoint a receiver in a pending action or proceeding. Petitioner moves generally under Code of Civil Procedure section 564(b) but does not specify which circumstance applies here. However, based on the papers, the court finds that Petitioner has made a sufficient showing under subsection (b)(1), "In an action [ ] between partners or others jointly owning or interested in any property or fund, on the application of the plaintiff, or of any party whose right to or interest in the property or fund, or the proceeds thereof, is probable, and where it is shown that the property or fund is in danger of being lost, removed, or materially injured." Case law warns that receivership, which takes a property out of the hands of its owners, should be invoked only when less severe options that would protect the rights of the parties, such as an injunction, are inadequate or unavailable. (See, Medipro Medical Staffing LLC v. Certified Nursing Registry, Inc. (2021) 60 c al.App.5th 622, 628; ?Alhambra-Shumway Mines, Inc. v. Alhambra Gold Mine Corp. (App. 3 Dist. 1953) 116 Cal.App.2d 869, 873.) Neither party discusses whether there are less severe alternatives to appointing a receiver to manage the business, or whether alternatives would or would not adequately protect the rights of the parties. Respondent suggests in the Opposition "If the Estate would cooperate either by substituting into the existing lawsuit or filing a petition in this Court to have its rights adjudicated, the actual value of both the LLC and the Estate's claims could be established. Appointment of a receiver is not an substitute [sic]." (Opp. at 3:25-27). Respondent does not include analysis in support of the quoted excerpt. Respondent has not demonstrated how filing a petition in this case number to ascertain the estate's interests (something Petitioner has already done) or substituting into a civil action would be an option less onerous than the requested receivership that would also protect the rights of the parties. The Petitioner's burden under Code of Civil Procedure section 564(b)(1) is to show by a preponderance of the evidence that (1) the estate has a probable joint interest in the property and (2) the property interest is in danger of loss or material injury. It does not matter if there is conflicting evidence, so long as a preponderance of that evidence supports the moving party's claims. [Part 2 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSEMARY GRACE TROWSDALE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. issues are the request to have the estate pay for preparing the probate accounting. an ordinary service included in the statutory fee of the personal representative (and would essentially be a request to classify that service as extraordinary), and the attorney request for anticipated additional fees for extraordinary services. Counsel may be asked for a legal basis to make this request in a petition for final distribution, filed once the probate administration is complete (final acts to close estate are included as ordinary services for which the attorney receives statutory fees). Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/2022 to allow time to amend the petition. The judicial officer will not approve a petition for final distribution that includes a petition to approve settlement. This petition must be amended to only request approval of a settlement agreement then a fully noticed petition for final distribution will have to be filed. Petitions to approve a settlement agreement must be filed separately and prior to any petition seeking distribution based on the terms of the settlement agreement and must include the following: 1) A citation to the Probate Code section under which the petition is presented; 2) The legal standard governing the petition; 3) A summary of the key terms of the settlement agreement; and 4) A statement regarding the parties' understanding of what they believe court approval means. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/6/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The decedent and petitioner never registered their domestic partnership with the California Secretary of State. Christina Montanes, the decedent's only child, consented to the relief sought by the petition, but died shortly before this petition was filed. Gina Montanes, Christina's sole heir as the surviving parent, filed her consent to the relief based on the petitioner's status as a putative registered domestic partner. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/07/2022 (no earlier date available). 1) The petition must be amended and renoticed to attach copies of both the will and codicil and identify each instrument as such. 2) The conservator of the estate or other legal representative of Carolyn Lewellen who is alleged to lack legal capacity must be identified and receive notice. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOIS MARIE HAYN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Jimmy Philippsen and Kerry Philippsen, the decedent's two nephews having priority for appointment, have not consented to the petitioner's appointment as administrator. 2) Kerry Philippsen, the decedent's nephew having priority for appointment, cannot be located. The petitioner requests that notice to Kerry Philippsen be dispensed with. The Court is to determine whether notice can be prescribed in any manner under CRC 7.52(c) or whether a guardian ad litem should be appointed for Kerry Philippsen as a person whose address is unknown under Probate Code section 1003(a)(5). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box.
